input.headline: In times of sweeping change, clarity matters most.

raw.headline: In times of sweeping change, clarity matters most.

winner: I told my GPS we're going through a time of sweeping change, so it better sweep me straight to the bathroom.

candidates.text: Why did the compass go into therapy? It had too much *change* and needed some *clarity*.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'change' and 'clarity' tie directly to the headline, are concise, surprising, and specific."}

candidates.text: Why did the compass break up with the GPS during the digital revolution? It said, "I need some *sweeping* clarity in this relationship—you're just too *changeable* for me!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative wordplay with 'sweeping clarity' and 'changeable' ties directly to the headline. Brevity and specificity are strong, with a natural flow and surprising twist."}

candidates.text: Why did I bring a map to the future? Because in times of sweeping change, even Google Maps can't keep up with my ability to get lost between "here" and "there.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Funny twist on the headline with clever wordplay and self-deprecating humor."}

candidates.text: Why did the compass get promoted? Because even during the greatest upheavals, it still knew which way was north—but now they wanted it to help everyone else figure out east, west, and south too. Sweeping change is nothing if not a team-building exercise.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.2, "reason": "Creative analogy with compass, ties to headline, good surprise and specificity"}

candidates.text: I told my GPS we're going through a time of sweeping change, so it better sweep me straight to the bathroom.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever and specific, using the headline's phrase in a humorous way with a twist involving GPS. It’s brief and surprising, though the humor is slightly situational and may not resonate as strongly with all audiences."}
